result.summary: Tiger Data offers a Tiger Cloud REST API, Tiger CLI, and Tiger MCP server, all authenticated with Tiger Cloud credentials (personal access token via Authorization: Bearer for REST; `tiger login` for CLI; MCP OAuth/login flow), plus an additional Tiger Cloud REST API preview surface with auth still unknown.

tiger_cloud_pat.label: Tiger Cloud personal access token
tiger_cloud_pat.setup: Go to the Tiger Cloud docs for the CLI/REST workflow and create a personal access token in your Tiger Cloud account. Use the token for API requests and CLI/MCP auth. The docs page is the easiest starting point: [Get started with the command line](https://www.tigerdata.com/docs/get-started/quickstart/cli-rest-api).

tiger_mcp_oauth.label: Tiger MCP OAuth login
tiger_mcp_oauth.setup: Use the Tiger MCP setup flow for AI assistants from the docs. The easiest path is to authenticate in the MCP/CLI setup flow described on [Integrate Tiger Cloud with your AI Assistant (MCP and CLI)](https://www.tigerdata.com/docs/get-started/quickstart/mcp-cli) and [Tiger MCP reference](https://www.tigerdata.com/docs/reference/tiger-cloud/tiger-mcp).
